accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurately describe its provisions, including the FTC enforcement and Stafford Act context.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; references to the FTC Act and disaster declarations are accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as prohibiting price gouging after a declared major disaster or emergency.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes violations are treated as unfair/deceptive acts under the FTC Act framework.

exceptions_conditions.why: Accurately captures the supplier-cost, tariff, hotel seasonal, and rental amortization exceptions.

effective_dates.why: Notes the 30-day, 180-day, and 2-year periods and that the prohibition takes effect on the declaration date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the price caps, exceptions, FTC/state/private enforcement, penalties, and fund deposit.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive without advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent across all sections.

concise.why: Tight summary without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the FTC, state attorneys general, and U.S. Attorney General consistent with the bill.

correct_figures.why: The 10%, 50%, 30-day, 180-day, $25,000, treble damages, and 2-year figures all match the bill.
